item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Saturday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur

greetings online friends. i hope you are having a frank and productive holiday season
everyone at work is coughing
bloodsport. one of my favorite movies
accurate
i would have had a skateboard, but yeah
far out
dumps on imgur
i can't think of a good caption for this one
no thank you
the whole outfit must stay on during sex
yes
eh? eh?? the... image before it.. nevermind
it doesn't taste like pineapple at all
i guess
facts
again?
no
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: Meme beats everything!
item.description:
Venom, the ultimate ally
This person deserves a Nobel prize
I may or may not have acquired a lot of images about one particular topic.
bless them all
Not to disparage the chief but. Where's Rom.
Yeah, I have a lot of these.
Yeah...
Me also
Avery's pah is strong.
My 3 brain cells.
No notes
But are they cute and cuddly, boys? Cute and cuddly?
King David and Alexander the Great were gay (not for each other... probably). There was a trans Roman emperor. People are people and for some reason weirdos have a problem with this.
Derek did nothing wrong... I assume
A lot of these were stolen from @CodyBurkett
Flesh and Blood TCG tax.

item.title: Sorry, Mario
item.dc:creator: ComradeBinch

item.title: For those feeling left out this month.
item.description: Boss and HR say "contractors do not get bonuses". Wife gets ghosted when she informs them she is on the salary with benefits. Even if she was a contractor, not getting even a card saying thanks for all the hard work is soul crushing. A cheap box of chocolates at least means they acknowledge your existence. He other jobs gave her cards saying thanks and that means a lot to her. She works so hard all the time, even going out to shovel snow with pneumonia at the school (I get up early to help as her coworker never come in to help in the morning). She had a rough go this year, being sick so much and hell, even having a partial retinal detachment. I'm sad people just take her for granted all the time, she is a great worker, fantastic mother and a caring wife.
item.dc:creator: MrNobodyLivesHere

item.title: Bottom shelf memes
item.description:
Dungeons and Dragon deez
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
I painted a canvas once...
And the internet wasn't happy, as usual.
So, last Sunday, my buddy Fukiplays invited us to watch him play as one of five contestants all competing for the heart of one woman named Rita on a live e-dating stream on Twitch. My husband, @FrothyStout along with a few other people from our server grabbed our popcorn and tuned into the stream to enjoy the chaos together. During the stream, Rita was asking all the contestants to rate her on a scale of 1-10. Apparently this was not an open question, because when I typed "Looks wise, she's an 8, but personality she's a 3."..she lost her goddamn mind. Although, I was only participating in the stream as a viewer, she made me feel like a real star when she spent TEN WHOLE MINUTES derailing the E-date to rant about ME, the Bottom™, who had the audacity to type anything in the twitch chat. You can see the full 10 minute meltdown https://youtu.be/dbGnkkiQtWc?si=wBBynpfz7xVv2L_P ..but here's an Imgur sized snippet.
The following evening, I decided to share the 10 minute video of her tantrum on https://twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka where she decided to make a surprise appearance to the watch party. I'll hand it to her though. She was a good sport throughout the video break down and stayed for the whole ride. For more context, that full video can be found https://youtu.be/UrYc0Xw5AdU?si=g0HdCh8rwwET-4KM
Join for the games. Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving degen there to play it with you. Join for the memes. We share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the NSFW memes. Join to ping @humlyhums Join to meet @crimebrulee the biggest problem in the server. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
Deaf cat Magoo (short for Oogala Magoogala) tax You can catch her live on https://twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: The Big Chonk Restoration: FINAL UPDATE!
item.description:
(*not actually the final update because I'm waiting on that marble slab but close enough for now. Reeee!) Happy Saturday, Imgur! It's been a busy three weeks since I last shared a progress post on this project and a lot has happened! For those keeping score, the last update post is over here: https://imgur.com/gallery/aUNt32W This is going to be a long post (lots of pictures to share) so I thought it'd be nice to do a quick recap of recent events to explain the delay. Starting with...
This little goober! A few weeks ago, shortly after posting the last update, this little kitty needed to go in for a dental surgery that required me to set aside work on the cash register so I could keep an eye on her as she recovered. Everything went well, and she's had no trouble finding her spunky inner self again, but for about a week Story was my sole focus and not much else. (Totally worth it, by the way.)
As for the cash register, it ended up sitting in this sorry state while I continued to fight a whole host of mechanical problems. Sticky keys, seized gears, the works. If one thing didn't want to work, ain't no way any of it was going to work. So while I did other things, this mech continued to get bukkakied daily with penetrating oil until it behaved.
I was also intermittently fiddling with the printer mechanism in the hopes that I might build up enough confidence to attempt disassembling it, but I ultimately backed away from that idea as I weighed the added stress and potential cost of botching it.
There was also the issue of the exterior panels, which sported all manner of rust, oxidation, grime, you name it. Thankfully most of this came off relatively easily with some WD-40 and steel wool!
(Like, nobody understands the black depths of my soul's ocean, man.) Yee, not as edgy as intended. At the end of my last update I mentioned I would be tackling the gunk that had crudded up the top of the drawer box. I talked to my dad about the issue, being that he's got several decades of woodworking experience, and he suggested scraping it off with a razor by drawing the blade across the wood. Sure enough, several dull blades later, it removed the majority of the grime to a point where I was satisfied. :)
With that done, I turned back to focus on finishing with the register mechanism. Namely, cleaning the layers of grime off all the little spots that would be a pain to clean once it was reassembled. The buttons are always a nasty spot to deal with.
But it turns out oven cleaner is excellent at loosening all that nastiness, so that's another tool in my belt! I'm not sure whether or not it's good for the celluloid so I didn't let it soak. Rather I spritzed it, wiped it off, spritzed again, and repeat.
Gotta say, the end result is hard to argue with.
I gave the side of the printer mechanism the same treatment, being careful not to flood any spots that would hold onto oven cleaner. Since these would be visible behind the printer box, I wanted them to at least look nice even if I don't plan to find paper or ink for it.
And now comes the fun part: reassembly! Starting with the cash box. To do this, I put the box on the bench. I really don't know what else to say about it.
The base plate comes next, looking much cleaner than it was before and sporting all of its battle scars in all their shiny glory.
Fun fact, I tweaked my back lifting the mech back onto the cash box! I'm never going to work on a register this large again! Wheee!
Can't do anything else until this whole thing gets bolted down, so... I bolted it down! That done, it's time to start assembling the bits and bobs onto the side panels.
Starting with the transaction counter! This little guy gets tripped each time the main sprocket performs a revolution, adding to the counter as it goes. That allows business owners to keep track of how many times the drawer was popped, since there's no way to open it otherwise on this model! Neat!
To date (assuming it was never reset) the drawer has been cycled 4300 times! That's super low, so I assume it was reset at least a few times in the last century or so.
That done, it's time to start slapping the chassis together. Something I'll mention is that the nickel plated registers really don't have that warm shine that the brass ones do, which is part of the reason why I tend not to gravitate toward these as readily. But with all its bells and whistles, this one has a definite industrial look that I like for much different reasons. Like the crown on the Empire State Building, minus the city.
Obvious next step: add the handle. All one hundred and twelve years of this machine's life are visible in how much chrome has been worn away by countless hands. History is awesome.
Flipping her over, the printer panels are next and they're looking a sight better than they did when they were coated in green death.
Can't forget the spool holder, and the pawl to the feed spool has to be screwed down as well.
Before the back goes on, the window needs to be reinstalled. Oh look, a cash drawer that still has to be finished! Wonder if that'll show up later on?
Bam! I ordered brand new glass to replace all the panes that had been chipped and cracked and they really give this machine a much needed face lift.
Ah yes. The back plates for each column of buttons. Each one takes three extremely tiny screws. Each screw really, really wants me to drop them into the machine. I cannot put into words how much focus it took me *not* to drop those tiny screws into the machine.
Quick topless shot of all those shiny plates reinstalled. :)
Next comes the counter cover. The slots stay covered unless someone turns a key, sliding the guard aside. Sadly, I don't have the key and finding the right one is a little more cash than I'm willing to spend. Guess I'm not manager material ;)
The front faceplate with its new glass goes up next, using the new fasteners I ordered off McMaster-Carr to replace the ones that were missing when I bought it.
Next, the main front body gets screwed down and the top... thingy. Hat? Top hat? I don't know what it's called. The top hat gets... un-doffed. I bolted it down. Leave me alone. :p
Can't forget the serial and model plate! Built the same year the Titanic turned into a submarine.
I nearly forgot this little guy! I'm fairly sure it's a guide for the receipt paper, though I'm only guessing. Either way, no extra parts allowed. Everything gets put back on!
Including this little gadget! This guy's the whole reason I decided to buy the register in the first place! I had no idea what it was for and I wanted to know, so... yeah, I bought a project that took over a month to finish just so I could figure out what this thing was. It prints extra numbers on the back of your receipt. (Worth it!)
Struggled a little with the number plate. Everything wanted to be cattywompus until I figured out the right order to screw it all down. Spring-loaded screws aren't very fun when they come flying at your face. @.@
And lastly, this little guy got put back where I found it, albeit a little less... gunky. It still has a few traces of discoloring, but that's fine by me. I made the mistake of polishing one of these once only to realize I had polished off all of the paint in the process. Rather to slightly too little than slightly too much!
Well alright then, that looks like an open and shut case, right? Right?? Hell no, we're not done yet! There's a whole ugly cash drawer to start on!
Aww yeah, look at that polish. That sheen. So perfect. Not a mark on it. Blugh. In the past, I've done a small bit of sanding on a couple of drawers just to smooth out the wood. The majority of the time, though, the drawers were in fine condition and just needed to be cleaned and oiled. This one... yeah.
This one isn't just worn out, it's worn through. The lacquer finish flakes off under my fingernail and the wood is dryer than the Sahara. It's *bad.* And it doesn't help that half of the tacks holding it together are rusted out , the wooden bottom is dry rotted, and one of the dividers was being held down with duct tape of all things. So... against my own screaming lack of confidence, I decided to attempt breaking this thing apart with the hope of refinishing it.
The dividers, thankfully, didn't put up much of a fuss. Turns out none of them were glued down. They were held together by tacks and joinery. Some of them took a little wiggling before they came out but there were ultimately no casualties. No casualties if you ignore the absolute foul condition of the bottom panel. Some of the wood was original, some was patched up with laminate strips which had also rotted away, and what was left was a flaking, uneven mess. It was bad enough that I couldn't pull it off the frame in one piece. It came off like dry corn husks.
One mess later and I have the bare frame. This, I was pretty sure, was glued together and I wasn't about to try to pull the pieces apart just for the sake of a full disassembly. The frame itself was in good condition. No cracks, no bending, just a flaking lacquer layer and some staining. So I left it intact as I continued on.
Levering this little guy off the front of the drawer was no easy task, but it eventually came away intact and only needed a small amount of rubber malleting to undo the bend I put into it. This, too, ended up getting slathered in some kind of lacquer that has since absorbed a century of tobacco smoke and grime. The problem is that this is too fragile to inflict on a buffing wheel and steel wool barely touched the lacquer layer at all. Instead, I ended up tacking down onto my workbench and wet sanded it back down to the brass by hand.
Speaking of sanding, that's what I did to the entire drawer (excluding the very front). All that lacquer came off like dry skin. It barely put up a fight. 60 grit, followed by 100 grit, and nothing more.
As for the ruined bottom panel, I took a trip out to the hardware store and picked up a sheet of quarter inch oak to replace it with. My dad gave me some advice to tape off the inside of my measurements before cutting to limit tearing along the edge.
Can't complain, it worked out pretty well!
That done, and after sanding the top of the new panel, I brought it all downstairs to stain.
I gave everything two coats with breaks in between. Ideally I'd have done this outside in the garage, but being that it's barely zero degrees outside and I don't have heat out there, the basement ended up being the spot to work. Luckily this Minwax didn't fumigate the whole house and the wood took the stain really well!
While the wood dried, I set all the rusted drawer hardware in an Evaporust bath and let it soak overnight. I'm playing with an alternative to this stuff, basically citric acid and laundry booster, but so far Evaporust still works out better. Once this gallon I have is used up, I'm going to set aside some time to zero in a cheaper alternative.
One night later and all that hardware looks a little less surface-of-Mars.
Time to see how it looks reassembled...
Aaand I can't complain! Nothing is tacked down just yet, but I'm happy with how it'll look once everything is wrapped up! A shallow light really does emphasize how deeply decades of coins gouged up the middle divider. I thought about replacing it for a few minutes, but I didn't think that would make much sense given that the rest of the cash register would be openly wearing its scars. So, that'll stay the way it is. Even if I end up fishing nickels out of the bill tray. :)
After wiping off the gunk that got trapped by the logo...
I tacked this bad boy back on and indulged in a good bit of staring. Very happy with how this cleaned up.
Next comes the hardware, and I have no idea why I zoomed in this close. My brain is kind of weird.
The roller and drawer catch go on next.
As well as the guide rail.
And voila, a fair bit of tacking later and this drawer is looking much better than I thought it would when I broke it apart! It's not perfect - the bottom panel is quite a bit lighter than the dividers - but who cares? It's not falling apart and that's good enough for a beginner!
Wahbam! That is one sexy looking chunker! And look at that, it seems to be *outside* of the workroom! Does that mean it's finally done? Is the title of this post actually true?
Nope! I'm still waiting on the marble slab to come in because the first one that I ordered didn't match my measurements, so it didn't fit! Hooray!
But until it arrives on Monday, this is the finished* product! A 1912 National Cash Register in nickel relief, torn down and rebuilt in its entirety. New glass, new fasteners, old panels, old scars. Hopefully a new marble slab to replace the one that's missing, and very likely destined to enjoy a spot in the sun somewhere in my house for a good while. And best of all, it WORKS again!
Though I do need to adjust one of the drawer springs so it's not going doink-a-doink when the drawer pops. ;) So... yeah! When I get the new slab put on, I'll post a quick final-final update, and that's it! Project finally done! It was an absolute beast to complete, fought me every step of the way, and gave me some very cool opportunities to learn skills I didn't have when I started. I call that a win! Thank you to all of you who have been following this project, sharing advice, and helping out with the costs. As always, if you'd like to help make these restorations happen, my Kofi is over at https://ko-fi.com/elbowdeepinahorse! Until the next project, have a happy holiday season to all you goobers! <3
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: No more eggplant!!
item.description: I went in for SRS on Friday, which is something that's been in the works for a while. It's been a little over three years since I officially came out, and 13 years since I really started questioning. It's been an interesting and rewarding journey and I don't expect that to change anytime soon.

item.title: I'm an editor and its getting BAD.
item.description: I'm a freelance editor that gets frequently hired by various publishers to essentially proofread novels-to-be and sweet googly moogly the amount of obviously AI shit is getting crazy. I can't name people or titles but I'm on my THIRD bible-sized "epic" fantasy story about generic LoTR rip off characters going on a checklist of predictable trope quests that reads like a 6 year old narrating a school play. I love fantasy and sci-fi but holy crap is this boring.
item.dc:creator: NappaTheFriendlyGhost

item.title: Commissions open!
item.description:
FOR 10% OFF! https://ko-fi.com/miagabriella/link/CHRISTMAS - Hey guys I’m sorry for this blatant ad for my commissions! I hope you can forgive me ❤️ I’m opening commissions again to earn some money! I’m happy to draw a loved one, your OCs…whatever you want! If youd like to inquire you can DM me there or on discord, I just go by Miaormoa! Did you know if you join my patreon as a free or paid member, you can also access my server? patreon.com/miaormoa
I have 5 different commissions available!
An example of some of my work!

item.dc:creator: AlphaPhoenix

item.title: He's suffered so much for so long
item.description: I'm sitting next to him while he does his nebulizer treatment. He stopped fighting back about that yesterday, except when he sees me through the plastic and tries to get to me. He stopped moving at all last night, except when I comfort him between syringe doses and he tries to climb to my face. He stopped eating three days ago, so I syringe feed him before giving him his antibiotics and putting him in the nebulizer box. He grooms himself still, but that's the only normal rat behavior he still exhibits. Other than trying to climb on me when he's scared, but that's probably the least comforting "normal" behavior. Unfortunately I get to see it a lot. He had a mild respiratory infection ten weeks ago that turned into a moderate ear infection six weeks ago that turned into a severe respiratory infection three weeks ago. The first two responded to treatment but a slight wheezing persisted and turned into a constant alternation between wheezing and desperate gasping. He does literally nothing else. He's a roughly 20 month old feeder rat. Optimistically, he could make it past 3 years, but he's a feeder so realistically I never expected more than 2-2.5 years. Feeders are kept in poor conditions that weaken their lungs when young, so unfortunately it makes sense that he can't shake this. (Remember kids, buy from breeders not feeders.) This is the last possible round of treatment before we need to do imaging. I've taken him to so many vets (often on an emergency basis) and got him so many medications... I can't afford to keep doing this, much less to intensify it. And I really think he's dying, no matter what I do. It's been such a slow burn infection that it's been treated not quite well enough multiple times and now seems antibiotic resistant. So, his next follow-up is in 9 days. If this round of treatment doesn't help him by then, I'll ask for him to be put down. He's not moving, he's not eating, he has to go through multiple hours of manhandling and uncomfortable treatments every day. And he's quite plausibly dying anyway. It kills me that his last few weeks are so clinical. Endless treatments and medicines. His food is a liquid formula administered via syringe. I can syringe feed him his favorite baby food too but the feeding makes the gasping worse and I think every time I've done that it's just made him more miserable. He isn't going to die happy. I feel so bad. The only times he shows signs of life are when he sees me. I can't tell if he's happy to see me or just desperate for comfort. Either way, he trusts me. I break down sobbing every time I do his syringing because he presses up against me and grabs me and looks at my face while he gasps and can't breathe in between doses, and when I whisper to him he calms down faster but I'm telling him it's going to be okay and he's such a good boy all while knowing I'm going to kill him. He trusts me so much and I'm going to kill him. Sorry for the long vent. I just needed to get this off my chest and I treat this place like a journal sometimes.
item.dc:creator: BearInASuit

item.title: Baby Elephants have no understanding of how absolutely massive they just behave like big dog
item.dc:creator: Inianquen

item.title: Let's send food to Imgurians in need
item.description:
It's time for TheFreePizzaDude! This week we unfortunately lost an important member of our team -
Today's post is dedicated to the memory of Nanna Pants If you'd like to help a struggling Imgurian get a meal in Nanna's honor you can find a request for food in the comments, reply to them and then send a DM to get an order set up Or you can contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and we'll send the food for you!
Anonymous Angels we all love you so much - https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude
And here's how you'd send it yourself! Any questions as an Angel? Just shoot us a message! We'll be on this username until 8pm central
Here is how it works for requests -
It's much easier for us to help requests in the US but we'll do our best to pick up requests from any country. Putting your country in your comment helps us match you to an Angel in your area
Any questions as a requestor? Just leave a comment! We'll have helpers on the post for 6 hours
14,710 meals since we started 928 grocery orders sent One old blind and deaf dog in our hearts Let's get it going! ... Today's Meal Tally 230 1st and 2nd by Pizza Angel @TheCraicen 315 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th by Pizza Angel @NoRoutesFound 325 7th, 8th, 9th and 10th by @OP 430 11th, 12th, 13th and 14th by @OP 520 15th by Pizza Angel @HelaSisterOfThor 530 16th and 17th by Pizza Angel @InterTakerWeb 555 18th and 19th by Pizza Angel @meisa1291 610 20th, 21st, 22nd and Michael Jordan by @OP 630 Kobe Bryant by Pizza Angel @kheltan 700 25th, 26th and 27th by Pizza Angel @IrvingSchwinglepuck 740 28th by Pizza Angel @pot314 800 29th and 30th by Pizza Angel @pot314 ... Today's Grocery Tally 250 1st by @OP 345 2nd by Grocery Angel @rawrberry 555 3rd by Grocery Angel @AyatollahBahloni 700 4th by Grocery Angel @BeardedHabilis 720 5th by Grocery Angel @TheCraicen 800 6th by Grocery Angel @AyatollahBahloni ... Alright everyone that's it for @OP Today we sent out 30 meals Filled 6 huge grocery orders That's 14,740 meals since we started 934 grocery orders sent Well done everyone, see you next week!
item.dc:creator: TheFreePizzaDude

item.dc:creator: chukyrlaw

item.title: Reposting but the sentiment stays rhe same
item.description: Edit: I'm gonna keep posting this all week. Everyone needs to know they're loved. I don't give a fuck about your faith, orientation, identity, gender, race, ethnicity, etc.... YOU FUCKING MATTER AND YOURE AWESOME BECAUSE YOU EXIST.
item.dc:creator: Syndicofberyl
